description: Compute the return on a leveraged position — amplifying gains and losses through borrowed capital at a cost

fields.name: borrowing_rate
fields.label: Interest rate on borrowed capital (decimal, same period as asset_return)

fields.name: leverage_ratio
fields.label: Total assets / equity (derived if not supplied)

core_formula.alternative_form: R_L = (V_E + V_B) / V_E * R_asset - (V_B / V_E) * r_D

amplification.gains: When R_asset > r_D, leverage amplifies gains
amplification.losses: When R_asset < r_D, leverage amplifies losses (including below cost of borrowing)
amplification.break_even: R_L = R_asset only when V_B = 0 (no leverage)

margin_mechanics.initial_margin: Regulatory / broker-set minimum equity percentage at trade initiation
margin_mechanics.maintenance_margin: Ongoing minimum equity; breach triggers margin call
margin_mechanics.margin_call: If equity / total_value < maintenance_margin, investor must deposit funds or liquidate

risk_considerations.unbounded_loss: Leveraged long can lose > 100% of equity if asset declines sharply
risk_considerations.cost_of_borrow_variable: If r_D floats (e.g., SOFR + spread), leverage cost changes with rates

negative_borrowing.description: Negative borrowed capital or borrowing rate

errors.message: Equity capital must be strictly positive
Borrowed capital and borrowing rate must be non-negative

anti_patterns.rule: Do not compute leveraged return without accounting for the cost of borrowing
anti_patterns.why: Ignoring borrowing cost overstates the true return delivered to equity holders

anti_patterns.rule: Do not apply a single borrowing rate when the broker's rate schedule is tiered or floating
anti_patterns.why: Tiered (size-dependent) or floating (SOFR + spread) rates produce materially different borrowing costs — use the actual schedule

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore margin call scenarios when projecting leveraged returns under stress
anti_patterns.why: A forced liquidation at market trough locks in losses that a deleveraged position would not experience

anti_patterns.rule: Do not compare leveraged returns to unleveraged benchmarks without risk adjustment
anti_patterns.why: Leverage multiplies both return and volatility; Sharpe ratio should be approximately preserved under leverage — if not, something else is going on

worked_example.scenario: Investor puts up 000 equity, borrows 000 at 4%. Asset returns 10%
worked_example.interpretation: 2x leverage turns a 10% asset return into 16% equity return; a -10% asset return would produce -24% equity return

asset_minus_50pct.interpretation: 50% loss on 2x leverage wipes out equity and creates debt to repay
